items.headline: Philippines' central bank complicates Binance's return to the country
items.subheadline: Binance and its local partner do not hold the necessary license required to operate in the country, the Philippine central bank said, according to local media reports.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Japan’s parliament poised to pass sweeping bill to regulate crypto like stocks
items.subheadline: The new rules, which are expected to come into effect in 2027, aim to foster innovation and crypto market growth to meet internal and external demand for digital asset services.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Why tokenization is an ETF-style market structure revolution
items.subheadline: The current tokenization dialogue and pattern resemble ETFs’ early days, which ultimately transformed into a $10+ trillion market, Lie argues.
items.author: Michael Lie

items.headline: Meta is paying creators in Stablecoins. Spending them is someone else's problem
items.subheadline: Meta’s decision to pay creators in USDC validates stablecoins as a mainstream disbursement tool, Joslyn suggests, but it also exposes the industry’s unresolved problem: moving seamlessly from digital dollars to usable local currency.
items.author: Tim Joslyn

items.headline: 5 corruption gaps Congress must close in the Clarity Act
items.subheadline: The most consequential crypto legislation in the world is moving forward in the U.S. Senate. As written, it leaves the United States exposed to money laundering, sanctions evasion, and conflicts of interest at the highest levels of government, argues Greytak.
items.author: Scott Greytak

items.headline: The quantum clock is ticking: it's Bitcoin's problem, not Ethereum's
items.subheadline: A recent research note published by Citi analysts reached a conclusion about quantum risk that should give every institutional bitcoin holder pause, Tabar explains.
items.author: Samir Tabar

items.headline: As SpaceX IPO approaches, Polymarket, Ventuals assign $2 trillion valuation: Crypto Daily
items.subheadline: Your day-ahead look for June 11, 2026
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Michael Saylor says Mnav is just one metric as Strategy dilution debate continues
items.subheadline: The two bitcoin treasury leaders renewed the debate over Strategy's mNAV and dilution, with Saylor arguing that equity issuance for cash strengthens, rather than dilutes, shareholders.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: Here's why bitcoin ETF outflows may have little to do with SpaceX mania
items.subheadline: Sygnum CIO Fabian Dori says recent bitcoin ETF outflows are more likely tied to arbitrage unwinds and macro liquidity pressures than investors rotating into anticipated IPOs such as SpaceX.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: OCC chief says Democrats applying sole political pressure in World Liberty charter choice
items.subheadline: The regulator rejected claims he's doing President Trump's bidding during a congressional hearing that also addressed the GENIUS Act stablecoin push.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Iran war shows markets no longer sleep
items.subheadline: The latest conflict involving Iran has produced an unexpected proving ground for financial infrastructure, and an unlikely winner has emerged, argues Huang.
items.author: Annabelle Huang

items.headline: Bitcoin spikes, then dumps, from $63,700 as analysts assess Strategy's next BTC moves
items.subheadline: BTC, ETH, XRP and others pulled back from their overnight highs as Iran-Israel tensions and oil rally triggered risk aversion in Asian stocks.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: BTC, ETH, SOL price news: Bitcoin has reached a deep bear-market valuation zone
items.subheadline: Two widely watched gauges show capitulation, but the analyst flagging them warns the slow grind comes next.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Trad.Fi, W3 target $650 million in onchain private credit using AI evaluation
items.subheadline: Equipment-financing lender Trad.Fi is working with W3 to automate capital workflows and bring real-economy business lending onto public blockchain rails.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Securitize CEO says tokenized stocks could unlock a $5 trillion crypto market
items.subheadline: At a panel at ETHConf, Carlos Domingo argued that bringing stocks and exchange-traded funds onchain could unlock a market far larger than today's roughly $30 billion tokenized asset sector.

items.headline: Japan's three largest banks eye joint stablecoin issue by March 2027
items.subheadline: MUFG, SMBC and Mizuho will establish a council to explore operational frameworks and prepare for the issuance of stablecoins.

items.headline: Mastercard prepares agentic commerce platform for a future where AI agents make payments
items.subheadline: The company is working with Coinbase, Stripe and other companies to build trusted payment systems for AI-driven commerce.

items.headline: Netomi CEO says $5 trillion AI customer experience market could boost stablecoin demand
items.subheadline: Puneet Mehta, a former high-level Wall Street engineer and data scientist, said the rapid expansion of AI enterprise software will increase demand for stablecoins and blockchain.

items.headline: Coinbase-backed Stand With Crypto calls on members to campaign against banks blocking digital asset transactions
items.subheadline: Stand With Crypto UK is telling its members to file formal complaints with high-street banks over sector-wide transfer bans.

items.headline: Singapore bank DBS to offer tokenized gold to retail customers
items.subheadline: Each token is backed by one gram of physical gold held by DBS in a dedicated vault in Singapore.

items.headline: XRP price news: Ripple-linked token above $1.10 as ETF inflows rise
items.subheadline: XRP bounced from recent lows on a late volume surge, though the token continued to lag the broader crypto market as futures traders positioned around key support levels.

items.headline: Bitcoin DAT buying collapses from $500 million per day to nearly negligible
items.subheadline: Bitcoin is losing institutional demand from two directions: Spot ETFs and digital asset treasuries.

items.headline: BTC updates: Bitcoin's price bounces ahead of the SpaceX IPO
items.subheadline: Fresh U.S. inflation data on Thursday was mixed, and the ECB hiked rates for the first time in nearly three years.

items.headline: BlackRock's income-paying bitcoin ETF nears launch at a fee that undercuts rivals
items.subheadline: The iShares Bitcoin Premium Income ETF makes money by selling call options on BlackRock's own IBIT.

items.headline: BTC price rises, holds above moving average signal that ETH, SOL can't penetrate

items.subheadline: The launch comes as AI agents increasingly emerge as participants in crypto markets, executing trades and managing capital on behalf of users.

items.subheadline: The decentralized identity project said attackers compromised the keys of a foundation member and are dumping the stolen H tokens for ether.

items.headline: Humanity's $36 million exploit happened because a 'multisig' lived on one laptop
items.subheadline: The compromised laptop held enough multisig keys to take over the project's bridges on two chains, a basic security failure for a startup backed by Pantera and Jump Crypto.

items.headline: Live BTC price updates: Bitcoin narrows early losses, returns to $62,000 as Nasdaq bounces to close down 1%

items.headline: Botanix bet big on 'Bitcoin DeFi.' Its shutdown suggests users never cared
items.subheadline: The project cited market conditions and a broader indifference among the cryptocurrency industry to establishing greater utility on the Bitcoin network.

items.subheadline: The move marks an integration of blockchain-based payments into a major live sporting and gaming event.

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: developers are exploring new token standards as privacy returns to focus

items.headline: The U.S. can’t lose the bitcoin race to China

items.headline: The agentic CFO in your pocket
items.subheadline: Chalom explains that retail investors have never had the opportunity to access and manage their own digital treasury desk. Until now.

items.headline: A bipartisan bridge to the future: Why the Senate must finish the job on digital Assets
items.subheadline: The Clarity Act’s recent markup proved that the momentum for regulation is there. It is imperative that Congress move the bill forward to establish rules this generation needs and a framework the next will inherit, urges Kim.

items.headline: Disciplined AI agents are the disruptor needed to break the exchange churn model
items.subheadline: Programmable incentives that allow independent trading agents to earn only when portfolios rise will create a fairer market for retail customers, explains Naja.

items.headline: What American crypto asset perpetuals mean for the future of crypto
items.subheadline: For years, one of the most significant crypto asset markets has existed entirely outside the United States. Today, that changes, explains CFTC Chairman Selig.

items.headline: How to better understand bitcoin’s perpetual identity crisis
items.subheadline: Here, Bailey unpacks the uncertainty surrounding the original cryptocurrency’s nature, which creates inconsistent market behavior.

items.headline: Crypto Clarity Act in spotlight for bad-actor provisions as Senate process grinds forward
items.subheadline: The industry has been trying to make a case this week the Clarity Act provides law enforcement strong tools to combat illicit finance involving cryptocurrency.

items.headline: U.S. House tax committee weighs crypto bills, including relief for small transactions
items.subheadline: Seven draft bills are being circulated by the House Ways and Means Committee ahead of a hearing next week, including proposals to ease small-gain, mining and staking burdens.

items.subheadline: The House Ways and Means Committee is gearing up for its big tax push.

items.headline: Sam Bankman-Fried officially asks Donald Trump for a presidential pardon

items.subheadline: The FCA suggested allowing UK UCITS schemes and some NURS to invest in crypto ETNs in its latest quarterly consultation paper.

items.headline: Crypto tax bills a work-in-progress as U.S. House lawmakers pose concerns
items.subheadline: The effort to push several tax bills is meant to be bipartisan, but the parties may not be comfortable with all details of the seven bills weighed by the panel.

items.subheadline: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission opened a proposed rule for public comment that sets an approach to determine contracts in the "public interest."
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Ondo Finance pushes into tokenized investment products, hires former Invesco ETF chief
items.subheadline: John Hoffman will spearhead Ondo's expansion from tokenizing individual assets to full investment portfolios and strategies.

items.subheadline: New legislation from a bipartisan duo would set up a multi-agency group under the U.S. attorney general to take a lead on cryptocurrency theft cases.

items.subheadline: A live banking trial in Switzerland alongside UBS and PostFinance is creating the blueprint for unified, bank-native stablecoin infrastructure.

items.subheadline: "Coinbase for Agents" is a new platform that lets AI assistants like ChatGPT and Claude connect to users’ Coinbase accounts to trade crypto, access data and eventually make payments and purchases autonomously.

items.headline: SpaceX's crypto-traded IPO was sharply falling. It now points upward to a $2.4 trillion valuation

items.subheadline: The former regulator joined a number of interest groups in arguing that prediction markets are overstepping their bounds by offering sports-related contracts.
